topic: K1 tidal period — luni-solar diurnal constituent
change_summary: detail_verdict_length grew 1199 → 1504 (+25%) since c3. Inspection of git log shows decider self-applied 3 successive patches on 2026-05-01 addressing the moderate (unverified monitor.py source claim) and minor (sidereal_day vs K1 framing) holes from c3. This c4 verifies the patches closed the holes and looks for new issues introduced by the rewrite.
our_argument_summary: K1 (23.9345 h) is the luni-solar diurnal tidal constituent, numerically equal to the sidereal day. The dome adopts the IERS catalogue value, displays pred=obs=23.9345 h on the Live Model Power dashboard, and labels it confirmed. Our review attacks on three vectors: (1) the value is trivial textbook physics catalogued since Doodson 1921 — no novel content, fails the post-hoc / trivial-transcription test (Part 3.1b); (2) the dashboard's pred=obs to four decimals plus the 'Source: IERS published' label demonstrates static catalogue lookup, not live measurement; (3) the dome's local moon at d/R ≈ 0.13 cannot generate the smooth global diurnal-inequality pattern that K1 is observed to produce — the tidal force collapses to ~9% at 5,000 km offset and ~1.48% at the equator, so the geometry contradicts the global signature. Verdict Self-Contradicted holds.

dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: Dashboard reports K1 with Predicted=23.9345 h, Observed=23.9345 h, Tolerance=0.01%, Status=PASS. Source label: 'IERS published'. The four-decimal exact match plus IERS-catalogue source label is decisive evidence of static-constant comparison rather than live measurement.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: Dashboard evidence shows hardcoded pred=obs comparison. monitor.py source itself is not in raw-text/ — however ISS-1546 (closed) and decider notes report direct inspection of monitor.py at 2026-04-25 confirmed M2 implementation as `m2_pred = LUNAR_H/2, m2_obs = 12.4206 as constants`. The K1 implementation is consistent with that pattern. The c3 review correctly noted raw-text/ does not contain monitor.py and recommended hedging the verdict prose; the decider applied that hedge in 13ee074/2b51d8a/77740f0.
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: 0.01% tolerance is technically tighter than the catalogue precision, but with hardcoded pred=obs the comparison is vacuous regardless.
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None remaining. The c3-flagged unverified-source-claim has been hedged. detail_evidence accurately describes the dashboard evidence ('exact agreement to four decimal places that occurs only when both values are copied from the same catalogue', 'Source: IERS published label is a self-admission').

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 216-217 shows Domain: K1 Tidal Period with Predicted=Observed=23.9345 h, tolerance 0.01%, source label 'IERS published'. Tag holds from c3.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: K1 catalogued by Doodson (1921). Standard tidal theory derives K1 from luni-solar declination forcing. Dome adopts the period verbatim and assigns aetheric mechanism. Same numerical prediction.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: K1 period tabulated for >100 years before V51.0 dome publication (2026). Trivial transcription per Part 3.1b.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula traces K1 from disc_radius, firmament_height, sun_altitude, or moon_altitude to 23.9345 h. The dome adopts the catalogue value; no derivation is offered.

holes_found.description: Prose redundancy in detail_verdict_text. The hedged sentence now mentions 'Source: IERS published' twice in close succession: once in the parenthetical ('per the dashboard Source: IERS published label — no independent code audit of monitor.py has been performed to verify this implementation detail directly') and again immediately after ('the dome's dashboard labels the source Source: IERS published, indicating the value comes from a fixed catalogue entry, not a live measurement'). The sentence also has stacked punctuation: an em-dash inside a parenthetical, followed by another em-dash continuing the outer clause. Readable, but tangled. A defender skim-reading might cite this as 'belaboring the point'.
holes_found.recommendation: Consolidate into one mention. Suggested replacement for the run from 'appear to be static constants' through 'not a live measurement': 'appear to be static constants with no live data fetch at runtime: the dashboard labels the source Source: IERS published — a fixed-catalogue lookup, not a live measurement. (No independent code audit of monitor.py has been performed to verify this implementation detail directly.)' This keeps the hedge intact, drops the redundant phrasing, and parenthesizes the audit caveat cleanly.

holes_found.description: Cross-WIN consistency gap. The c3 review's moderate finding explicitly recommended 'Apply same fix to WIN-045 and WIN-046 boilerplate' because all three WINs share the same monitor.py-variable-name boilerplate (WIN-045: 'LUNAR_H/2 = 24.84/2'; WIN-046: 'SOLAR_H/2 = 24.00/2'; WIN-049: previously 'sidereal_day constant'). The decider patched WIN-049 only. WIN-045 and WIN-046 still contain the original unhedged claim 'are hardcoded static constants in monitor.py with no live data fetch at runtime'. A defender reading the three tidal WINs in sequence sees an inconsistent rhetorical posture: WIN-049 says 'appear to be' with an explicit caveat about no source-code audit, while WIN-045/046 assert specific variable identifiers without caveat. NOTE: ISS-1546 (closed) appears to have verified the variable-name claims by direct monitor.py inspection at 2026-04-25 — if that inspection is documented and reproducible, the better fix is to ADD a citation to all three WINs (preferred path (a) from c3) rather than hedge WIN-049 backward. Either way, WIN-045/046/049 should land in the same posture.
holes_found.recommendation: EITHER (a) locate the monitor.py inspection artifact referenced by ISS-1546, cite it in all three WINs (a commit SHA, file path, or raw-text snippet), and revert WIN-049 to the unhedged form — preferred because the c3 hedge was a fallback for the case where source could not be cited; OR (b) propagate the WIN-049 hedge boilerplate to WIN-045/046, replacing the specific variable identifiers ('LUNAR_H/2 = 24.84/2', 'SOLAR_H/2 = 24.00/2') with phrasing parallel to WIN-049's current form. Path (a) is stronger if the artifact is recoverable. Suggest opening an issue against WIN-045 and WIN-046 with this as the fix scope.

stronger_arguments.description: The dashboard's 0.01% tolerance for K1 (23.9345 vs 23.9345) is itself a tell. IERS K1 catalogue precision is typically 4–5 significant figures. A 0.01% tolerance band on a 4-decimal value means the comparison cannot fail unless someone manually changes the constants — exactly what 'hardcoded' implies. detail_evidence already makes the four-decimal-match argument; it could optionally add the tolerance-vs-precision observation as a complementary tell.

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome model is correct that K1 ≈ 23.9345 h matches the sidereal day to 5 decimals. This is a real numerical fact — K1 is defined to be locked to the sidereal frame because the constituent arises from luni-solar declination forcing modulated by Earth's rotation. The dome did not invent this; it correctly reports it.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Reporting a centennially-tabulated catalogue constant correctly is not a prediction. The dome's claim under its own definition ('a number derived from the model, registered with a timestamp, and subsequently confirmed by independent data') requires derivation from dome geometry. No such derivation exists — the value is adopted from the catalogue and labeled confirmed. This is the trivial-transcription case Part 3.1b explicitly enumerates.

deeper_analysis: The c3→c4 trajectory is a textbook case of the decider's self-apply patch loop closing curmudgeon-flagged holes. The moderate hole (unverified source-code claim) was hedged using fallback option (b) — replace specific identifier with safe phrasing about the dashboard label, plus an explicit no-source-audit caveat. The minor hole (sidereal_day vs K1 framing) was bridged with 'the sidereal day / K1 tidal period, ~23.934 h'. Both fixes are correct. The only carryover concern is consistency: WIN-049's three sibling WINs in the same boilerplate cluster (WIN-045/046, plus WIN-050/051 which already have a different boilerplate because monitoring='none' for those constituents) were not patched in parallel. This c4 surfaces that as a moderate cross-WIN finding — the c3 review explicitly called out the boilerplate-shared vulnerability and the patch did not propagate. The other minor finding (prose redundancy from the hedge insertion) is cosmetic and easily fixed in a follow-up patch.

citations_verified: Doodson (1921) DOI 10.1098/rspa.1921.0088 — verified in c2 and c3, stable.
Section 3.1b 'trivial-transcription test' — verified in sections.json (part3 / Section 3.1b).
showTab('selftest') cross-link to Section 2.2 — link target exists.
Dashboard pred=obs=23.9345 h — verified at raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 216-217.
Dashboard 'Source: IERS published' label — verified at raw-text/08-live-power.txt line 217 region (label is the rendered Source field on the dashboard panel).
citations_unchecked: K1 open ocean amplitude '~14 cm' in detail_evidence — equilibrium amplitude is ~16 cm (Doodson coefficient 0.36505); observed varies 10–50 cm regionally. The '~' qualifier survives this. Carried over from c2/c3, low priority.

ISS-1546's claim that monitor.py was directly inspected at 2026-04-25 and shown to define `m2_pred = LUNAR_H/2, m2_obs = 12.4206 as constants` — closed-issue note exists but the underlying inspection artifact (e.g., a curmudgeon review file or a quoted snippet) was not located in this run. If recoverable, it would justify reverting WIN-049's hedge per the cross-WIN finding's path (a).

advocate_mode.best_defense: Reading as a fair-minded dome defender accepting the review's six methodological principles: 'Your hedge backed off the monitor.py claim because you couldn't read the source. But you're still asserting hardcoded based purely on the rendered dashboard. Source: IERS published is just a citation indicator, not proof of static comparison — the dashboard could be rendering values that came from a runtime fetch of the IERS Bulletin. Your evidence is now weaker than your conclusion.' Defender attempts to exploit the gap between observable evidence (dashboard) and the structural claim (no live fetch).

cross_win_consistency.inconsistencies_found: WIN-049 currently uses the hedged language 'appear to be static constants ... per the dashboard Source: IERS published label — no independent code audit of monitor.py has been performed to verify this implementation detail directly'. WIN-045 and WIN-046 still use the unhedged original boilerplate 'are hardcoded static constants in monitor.py' with specific variable identifiers ('LUNAR_H/2 = 24.84/2', 'SOLAR_H/2 = 24.00/2'). WIN-050/051 are differently-shaped because monitoring='none' for O1/N2 (no monitor.py domain at all) so their boilerplate explicitly says 'no monitor.py entry exists'. The three WINs that DO claim a monitor.py implementation (045/046/049) are now in two different rhetorical postures. The c3 finding explicitly anticipated this and recommended propagation; the patch propagation did not occur. Captured in this c4 as a moderate hole.

claims_checked: d/R ≈ 0.13 with d=moon altitude=2,534 km, R=disc radius=20,015 km
Tidal force drops to ~9% at 5,000 km offset from sub-lunar point
Tidal force drops to ~1.48% at the equator (~10,000 km offset)
quantitative_verification.computations: K1 catalogued period = 23.9345 h ≈ 23.93 h ✓. d/R = 2534/20015 = 0.1266 ≈ 0.13 ✓. At 5000 km offset, line-of-sight to moon = sqrt(2534² + 5000²) = 5605.4 km; force ratio (1/r²)·(2534/5605)² approximately the tidal-force-fall-off (1/r³ relative to point-source dominant tidal term) → (2534/5605.4)³ = 0.0925 = 9.25% ≈ 9% ✓. At equator (≈10,008 km), r = sqrt(2534² + 10008²) = 10324 km; force ratio = (2534/10324)³ = 0.01481 = 1.48% ✓. K1 amplitude '~14 cm' open ocean — equilibrium 16 cm, observed varies 10–50 cm regionally; '~14' falls in the lower regional range, hedge holds.

summary_for_decider: Patches landed cleanly. Verdict holds. Two findings: minor prose redundancy in the hedged sentence (cosmetic), and a moderate cross-WIN consistency gap — c3 explicitly recommended applying the WIN-049 hedge (or a verifiable cite) to WIN-045/046 in the same boilerplate cluster, but the patch only landed on WIN-049. Recommend opening an issue against WIN-045/046 to propagate, ideally via the path-(a) artifact-cite route since ISS-1546 reportedly verified the variable names by direct monitor.py inspection at 2026-04-25. If the inspection artifact is recoverable, WIN-049 could even revert its hedge.
